refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was mostly atomic, focusing on meeting or exceeding a customer-defined availability target. The refinement clarifies the requirement by specifying the measurement context (operational availability) and the evaluation period, ensuring the FR remains solution-neutral and focused on what the system must achieve.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the operational availability of the NGHE solution over the agreed evaluation period and compare it against the customer-defined target to verify compliance.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall achieve an operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target over the agreed evaluation period.

candidate_dps: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability
Incorporate real-time system health monitoring and predictive maintenance
Design modular subsystems to isolate faults and enable quick repair
Establish operational procedures and safety plans to minimize downtime
Use adaptive resource allocation to maintain performance under varying conditions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability

potential_sources: Redundancy mechanisms tightly coupled with system control logic
Monitoring systems dependent on specific hardware implementations
coupling_check.notes: Selected DP focuses on logical redundancy to achieve availability independently of other DPs. Care should be taken to design redundancy mechanisms that do not create tight coupling with control or monitoring systems.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that redundancy can be implemented without excessive cost or complexity
Risk that operational policies may not be fully effective in minimizing downtime
Assumes that measurement of availability is accurate and consistent over evaluation period
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a DP for real-time health monitoring to complement redundancy and improve fault detection and repair times, enhancing overall availability.

im_boundary.name: Safety Services
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for ensuring system safety, including failover and redundancy mechanisms

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Used to notify other modules or systems of safety-related events or failover activations

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives system status data and sends back health and failover status information

module_containment_scores.notes: Very broad module; contains many unrelated functions, leading to high diffusion of failover logic across unrelated components.
Contains physical components and some system logic, but failover is more safety-related than vehicle-specific; moderate diffusion expected.
Focused on remote control; failover mechanisms are not central here, leading to poor containment and high diffusion.
Highly cohesive for failover and redundancy; minimal diffusion outside this module expected.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The failover capability is best contained within the Safety Services module, as it directly relates to system safety and redundancy, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may require careful interface management.
If failover mechanisms require coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, some cross-module communication complexity may arise.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Implement redundant system components to ensure failover capability' directly addresses the FR of achieving operational availability equal to or greater than the customer-defined target by providing failover mechanisms to maintain system uptime.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may require careful interface management.
If failover mechanisms require coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules, some cross-module communication complexity may arise.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The Safety Services module shows high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2) for failover and redundancy logic, indicating strong cohesion and minimal diffusion risk. Other modules show higher diffusion scores, supporting the choice of Safety Services as the primary IM for this DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of the FR through the Safety Services module is feasible by monitoring system availability metrics (≥0.97) and ensuring failover events are correctly managed and reported via the SafetyAlert and Telemetry interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the failover capability DP to the Safety Services IM is clear and well-justified, with explicit module boundaries and interfaces defined to support implementation and verification.

recommended_revisions: Explicitly document interface contracts between Safety Services and other modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation) to manage dependencies and reduce cross-module communication complexity.
Include detailed telemetry data requirements and failover event reporting protocols in the Safety Services module specification to ensure clear verification paths.
Consider periodic review of module containment and diffusion scores as system evolves to maintain architectural integrity and minimize diffusion risks.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with strong justification and verification feasibility. The DP and IM selections directly address the FR with clear module boundaries and manageable diffusion risks. Recommended revisions focus on documentation and interface clarity rather than fundamental decomposition changes. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining architectural integrity is crucial, but no further sub-FR decomposition is warranted at this stage.

revise.why: To explicitly document interface contracts and telemetry data requirements between Safety Services and other modules to manage dependencies and reduce cross-module communication complexity, enhancing verification and architectural clarity.

assumptions: The Safety Services module can effectively contain failover and redundancy logic with minimal diffusion risk.
Telemetry data dependencies and cross-module communications can be managed through well-defined interfaces.
Verification of system availability metrics and failover event reporting is feasible via the Safety Services module.
open_risks: Potential dependency on telemetry data from other modules may introduce interface complexity.
Cross-module communication complexity if failover mechanisms require coordination with Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.
evidence_needed: Detailed interface contracts between Safety Services and other modules.
Telemetry data requirements and failover event reporting protocols.
Periodic containment and diffusion score reviews as system evolves.
